opinion.h4: FOSDICK v. LINZELL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OHIO. No. 704. Decided April 6, 1959.
opinion.p: The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for certiorari, certiorari is denied.
MR. JUSTICE STEWART took no part in the consideration or decision of this case.
BRISTOL ET AL. v. HEATON ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF CIVIL APPEALS OF TEXAS, TENTH SUPREME JUDICIAL DISTRICT. No. 581, Misc. Decided April 6, 1959.
Will Wilson, Attorney General of Texas, James N. Ludlum, First Assistant Attorney General, and Leonard Passmore and John Reeves, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for certiorari, certiorari is denied.
